findings.fingerprint: .pi/workflows/github-pr-review.yaml:218:QUALITY:Severity selector uses exact case-sensitive match, breaking mark-fix-attempted

issue.title: Severity selector uses exact case-sensitive match, breaking mark-fix-attempted
issue.problem: The new `mark-fix-attempted` node selects findings with `severity: "{{inputs.fixSeverity}}"`. `inputs.fixSeverity` is lowercase (e.g. "high"), but review findings store severity as uppercase ("HIGH", "CRITICAL", etc.) and `review-threshold` treats severity as a minimum rank. The `selectorMatches` helper performs an exact, case-sensitive string comparison, so this node will match zero findings and fail to record that fixes were attempted. It also leaves higher-severity findings (e.g. CRITICAL when fixSeverity=HIGH) unmarked.
issue.solution: Add a `minSeverity` selector that normalizes case and compares severity ranks. In `src/lib/review-artifact.ts`, add a rank helper (CRITICAL=4, HIGH=3, MEDIUM=2, LOW=1), add `minSeverity?: string` to `ReviewFindingSelector`, and update `selectorMatches` to return `severityRank(finding.issue.severity) >= severityRank(selector.minSeverity)` when `minSeverity` is set. Then change the workflow line from `severity: "{{inputs.fixSeverity}}"` to `minSeverity: "{{inputs.fixSeverity}}"`.

issue.title: Unwrapped JSON.parse in review-artifact-add-finding node
issue.problem: `getReviewIssueFromNode` calls `JSON.parse(issueName)` directly when the `with.issue` value is not an artifact name. If the value is not valid JSON, a raw `SyntaxError` bubbles up instead of the intended workflow node error, making debugging harder.

findings.fingerprint: src/cli/workflow.ts:1489:QUALITY:isReviewIssue type guard misses required agent field

issue.title: isReviewIssue type guard misses required agent field
issue.problem: The new `isReviewIssue` type guard checks `severity`, `category`, `title`, `file`, `problem`, and `solution`, but `ReviewIssue` also requires a non-optional `agent: string`. An object without `agent` can pass the guard, be treated as a valid `ReviewIssue`, and later cause undefined-agent errors when the artifact is rendered or posted.
issue.solution:
Add `typeof issue.agent === 'string'` to the return expression in `isReviewIssue`:
```ts
return (
  typeof issue.severity === 'string' &&
  typeof issue.category === 'string' &&
  typeof issue.title === 'string' &&
  typeof issue.file === 'string' &&
  typeof issue.problem === 'string' &&
  typeof issue.solution === 'string' &&
  typeof issue.agent === 'string'
);
```
